completed_tasks.name: Separate About and Contact Us routes
Upgrade the launcher typography and color system
Finish visual parity between public launcher and authenticated dashboard
Implement login-required Free Basic versus Premium access gating
Add backend-owned subscription sync and upload quota enforcement

remaining_tasks.name: Connect a real Supabase/Stripe environment for hardening verification
Define production deployment target and release workflow

blockers.description: Production deployment target and release process are not yet defined in-repo.
blockers.workaround: AWS Amplify is now the chosen host and repo config exists, but the actual Amplify app/environment still needs to be created and validated.

human_actions_pending.action: Confirm the production deployment target and release workflow.
human_actions_pending.context: AWS Amplify is selected, but launch-readiness still needs the actual Amplify environment, secret wiring, and release ownership path.

decisions.decision: Keep the signed-in dashboard as the unlocked continuation of the public launcher.
decisions.rationale: Visual parity stays strong while adding live preferences and clearer document detail.

decisions.decision: Use explicit authorized preview/download language and realistic placeholder states instead of generic file buttons.
decisions.rationale: The web surface needs to distinguish metadata browsing from protected file access honestly.

decisions.decision: All users must authenticate before using the product.
decisions.rationale: Account identity, recovery, and plan enforcement all depend on login being universal.

decisions.decision: Free Basic is limited while premium unlocks the broader cloud-backed workspace.
decisions.rationale: This gives the product a clear upgrade boundary without hiding the account model.

decisions.decision: Use email-based account recovery and recovery-capable encryption language, not zero-knowledge claims.
decisions.rationale: The product promise is recovery under pressure, so strict zero-knowledge positioning would over-constrain real recovery UX.

next_action: Create the real AWS Amplify app/environment and then perform the remaining Phase 6 hardening, audit, and launch-readiness verification.
context_notes: Phases 4 and 5 are implemented and locally verified. Local client and server secrets are configured for Supabase and Stripe, the Supabase MCP server is authenticated, the Stripe Codex plugin is installed, and the repo now has an Amplify build contract. The remaining blocker is live environment validation.
